chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP No.D–2536 of 2025 ________________________________________________ DATE: ORDER WITH SIGNATURE(s) OF JUDGE(s) ________________________________________________ 1. For order on office objection No.1 & 2 2. For hearing of main case 22-07-2025 M/s. Nazir Ahmed Memon & Shahid Nazeer, Advocates for Petitioner a/w Petitioner (on bail). Mr. Ali Haider Saleem, APG --------------- This Bail Petition has been filed on the ground that the allegations made in FIR No.107 of 2025 registered at police station Mirpur Bathoro District Sujawal under Section 93 of the Control of Narcotic Substances Act, 2024, are incorrect and nothing has been recovered from the possession of the Petitioner. The Petitioner approached the trial Court in FIR No.108 of 2025 where his bail has already been confirmed and in the instant case, bail application was not entertained for want of jurisdiction. 2. The Bail Petition is opposed by the Additional Prosecutor General Sindh on the ground that the Petitioner is nominated in FIR with specific allegation that he thrown 1130 grams Charas, therefore, he is not entitled for any relief. 3. Heard learned Counsel for the Petitioner, learned Additional Prosecutor General Sindh and perused the record. 4. From perusal of record, it appears that nothing was recovered from possession of Petitioner and only allegations against the Petitioner is that he thrown 1130 grams Charas. No private witness is cited in this case. The question as to whether the contraband has been thrown by the Petitioner or not, shall be determined through evidence by the trial Court after recording evidence, however, at this stage, the Petitioner has made out a case for confirmation of bail.

No private witness is cited in this case. The question as to whether the contraband has been thrown by the Petitioner or not, shall be determined through evidence by the trial Court after recording evidence, however, at this stage, the Petitioner has made out a case for confirmation of bail. Therefore, the interim pre-arrest bail already granted to the Petitioner vide order dated 03.06.2025 is hereby confirmed on same terms and condition. The Petition stands disposed of accordingly. JUDGE JUDGE FAIZAN/
